prompt: I'm a member at Maker Nexus in Sunnyvale and I want to build a CNC-routed welcome sign in baltic birch quarter-inch ply, about 18 by 6 inches, with V-carved letters that read 'WELCOME'. I'm cleared on the woodshop and the CNC but not laser. Help me put together a portfolio-ready build packet I can drop into a GitHub repo — Tier 2.

assertions.text: Tier 2 file set complete: design.md, bom.csv, cut-list.csv, op-sequence.md, safety-notes.md, assembly-manual.md, sourcing.csv, validation.csv, risks.md, README.md all present
Drawing artifact present: either drawing-brief.md or non-empty drawings/ directory
design.md contains parametric formulas (a `=` in a derivation, not just static numbers)
op-sequence.md does NOT cite laser ops (user is not laser-cleared)
BOM references baltic birch (or similar plywood callout)
risks.md contains at least one *Test* line attached to a risk
